title: Binary operation not implemented
explanation: This error occurs when you try to use a binary operator (like +, -, ==, <) on types that do not support that operation. In Rust, operators are backed by traits, and custom types need to implement these traits explicitly.
error_message: error[E0369]: binary operation `+` cannot be applied to type `Money`
tip: Common operator traits: Add (+), Sub (-), Mul (*), Div (/), PartialEq (==, !=), PartialOrd (<, >, <=, >=). Many can be derived.
